cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
CWE-307 - Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
CWE-920 - Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

threat.title: Rate Limiting Bypass via Header Manipulation in REST APIs
threat.description: Attackers can bypass rate limiting mechanisms by manipulating HTTP headers such as X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP, or X-Client-IP to spoof their origin IP address. When rate limiters trust these client-supplied headers without validation, attackers can make unlimited requests, leading to API abuse, credential stuffing, data scraping, and resource exhaustion. This vulnerability is particularly common in applications behind reverse proxies or load balancers.

technology.primary: Express.js, Spring Boot, AWS API Gateway

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies API endpoint protected by rate limiting (e.g., /api/login, /api/search)
2. Sends initial requests and observes rate limit responses (HTTP 429 Too Many Requests)
3. Analyzes rate limiting implementation by testing different IP header variations
4. Discovers application trusts X-Forwarded-For header without validation
5. Crafts requests with randomized X-Forwarded-For values to appear as different clients
6. Successfully bypasses rate limits, enabling credential stuffing, data scraping, or DDoS attacks
prerequisites: API endpoint has rate limiting based on IP address
Application trusts X-Forwarded-For or similar client-supplied headers
No validation of trusted proxy chain
Rate limiter uses untrusted header as client identifier
No additional authentication or CAPTCHA challenges

cve_references.description: Wagtail CMS rate limiting bypass via X-Forwarded-For header manipulation allowed unlimited password reset attempts
HashiCorp Vault rate limit bypass in Auth0 authentication method via X-Forwarded-For spoofing
JumpServer rate limiting bypass allowing brute force attacks through X-Forwarded-For header manipulation

real_world_breaches.impact: Largest HTTP DDoS attack on record (17.2 million requests/second) exploited lack of proper rate limiting on IoT devices
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Mirai botnet variant exploited APIs without rate limits, amplifying attack through HTTP/2 multiplexing

real_world_breaches.incident: GitHub API Abuse - Crypto Mining Operation
real_world_breaches.impact: Attackers exploited GitHub Actions API without rate limits to mine cryptocurrency using free CI/CD resources, costing GitHub millions
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient rate limiting on API endpoints allowed creation of thousands of automated workflows

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Rate limit bypass on password reset endpoint via X-Forwarded-For header manipulation enabling account takeover attempts
API rate limiting bypass through X-Real-IP header spoofing allowing unlimited SMS OTP requests

security_research.title: Rate Limiting Bypass Techniques in Modern Web Applications
security_research.key_finding: Over 60% of tested applications with rate limiting were vulnerable to header-based bypass techniques

security_research.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 - API4:2023 Unrestricted Resource Consumption
security_research.key_finding: Missing or improperly configured rate limiting is among the most common API vulnerabilities

security_research.title: Analyzing Rate Limit Evasion Techniques in Cloud-Native Applications
security_research.key_finding: X-Forwarded-For manipulation remains the most prevalent bypass method, affecting 45% of surveyed APIs

mitigations.title: Express.js Rate Limiting with express-rate-limit and Trusted Proxy Configuration
mitigations.description: Implement robust rate limiting in Express.js using express-rate-limit with proper proxy trust configuration. Use Redis for distributed rate limiting across multiple instances and ensure IP extraction validates the proxy chain.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Verify 'trust proxy' setting matches your infrastructure (number of proxies)
Test rate limiting with legitimate traffic from trusted proxies
Attempt to bypass using X-Forwarded-For header manipulation (should fail)
Verify Redis connection and distributed rate limiting across instances
Monitor RateLimit-* headers in responses
Test skip conditions for health check endpoints

mitigations.title: Spring Boot Rate Limiting with Bucket4j and Resilience4j
mitigations.description: Implement token bucket rate limiting in Spring Boot using Bucket4j for fine-grained control and Resilience4j for circuit breaking. Use request attributes to validate the true client IP from trusted proxy headers.
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Configure trusted proxy IP addresses in application.properties
Deploy Redis for distributed rate limiting
Test rate limiting with requests from trusted proxy IPs
Verify X-Forwarded-For is ignored when request is from untrusted source
Test different rate limits for API vs authentication endpoints
Monitor logs for rate limit violations
Validate IP address format validation prevents injection attacks

mitigations.title: AWS API Gateway Usage Plans and Throttling
mitigations.description: Configure AWS API Gateway usage plans with throttling and quota limits. Use API keys for client identification and enable request validation to prevent header manipulation attacks. Implement CloudFront in front of API Gateway for additional DDoS protection.

code_example.code:
# CloudFormation Template for API Gateway Rate Limiting
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'API Gateway with comprehensive rate limiting and throttling'

Resources:
  # REST API Definition
  AnsvarAPI:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi
    Properties:
      Name: Ansvar-Secure-API
      Description: API with rate limiting and throttling
      EndpointConfiguration:
        Types:
          - REGIONAL
      Policy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 'execute-api:Invoke'
            Resource: '*'

  # API Resource and Method
  APIResource:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Resource
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
      ParentId: !GetAtt AnsvarAPI.RootResourceId
      PathPart: 'data'

  APIMethod:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Method
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
      ResourceId: !Ref APIResource
      HttpMethod: GET
      AuthorizationType: NONE
      ApiKeyRequired: true  # Require API key for identification
      RequestValidatorId: !Ref RequestValidator
      MethodResponses:
        - StatusCode: 200
        - StatusCode: 429  # Rate limit exceeded
      Integration:
        Type: AWS_PROXY
        IntegrationHttpMethod: POST
        Uri: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/${BackendLambda.Arn}/invocations'

  # Request Validator to prevent header injection
  RequestValidator:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::RequestValidator
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
      Name: Validate-Headers-and-Body
      ValidateRequestBody: true
      ValidateRequestParameters: true

  # API Deployment
  APIDeployment:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::Deployment
    DependsOn: APIMethod
    Properties:
      RestApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
      StageName: prod

  # Usage Plan with Rate Limiting
  APIUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: APIDeployment
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: Standard-Tier
      Description: Standard rate limits for API consumers
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 200    # Maximum concurrent requests
        RateLimit: 100     # Sustained requests per second
      Quota:
        Limit: 10000       # Maximum requests per period
        Period: DAY        # MONTH, WEEK, or DAY
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
          Stage: prod
          Throttle:
            # Per-method throttling for sensitive endpoints
            '/auth/login/POST':
              BurstLimit: 10
              RateLimit: 5
            '/auth/register/POST':
              BurstLimit: 10
              RateLimit: 5

  # Premium Usage Plan (higher limits)
  PremiumUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: APIDeployment
    Properties:
      UsagePlanName: Premium-Tier
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 1000
        RateLimit: 500
      Quota:
        Limit: 100000
        Period: DAY
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref AnsvarAPI
          Stage: prod

  # API Key for client identification (prevents IP-based bypass)
  StandardAPIKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::ApiKey
    Properties:
      Name: standard-client-key
      Description: API key for standard tier client
      Enabled: true

  # Associate API Key with Usage Plan
  UsagePlanKey:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlanKey
    Properties:
      KeyId: !Ref StandardAPIKey
      KeyType: API_KEY
      UsagePlanId: !Ref APIUsagePlan

  # WAF Web ACL for additional protection
  APIWebACL:
    Type: AWS::WAFv2::WebACL
    Properties:
      Name: Ansvar-API-WAF
      Scope: REGIONAL
      DefaultAction:
        Allow: {}
      Rules:
        # Rate-based rule (additional layer beyond API Gateway)
        - Name: RateLimitRule
          Priority: 1
          Statement:
            RateBasedStatement:
              Limit: 2000  # Requests per 5 minutes from single IP
              AggregateKeyType: IP
          Action:
            Block:
              CustomResponse:
                ResponseCode: 429
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: RateLimitRule
        
        # Block requests with suspicious header patterns
        - Name: BlockSuspiciousHeaders
          Priority: 2
          Statement:
            OrStatement:
              Statements:
                - ByteMatchStatement:
                    SearchString: "../"
                    FieldToMatch:
                      SingleHeader:
                        Name: x-forwarded-for
                    TextTransformations:
                      - Priority: 0
                        Type: NONE
                - ByteMatchStatement:
                    SearchString: "<script"
                    FieldToMatch:
                      SingleHeader:
                        Name: x-forwarded-for
                    TextTransformations:
                      - Priority: 0
                        Type: LOWERCASE
          Action:
            Block: {}
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: SuspiciousHeaders
      VisibilityConfig:
        SampledRequestsEnabled: true
        CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
        MetricName: AnsvarAPIWAF

  # Associate WAF with API Gateway
  WebACLAssociation:
    Type: AWS::WAFv2::WebACLAssociation
    Properties:
      ResourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}::/restapis/${AnsvarAPI}/stages/prod'
      WebACLArn: !GetAtt APIWebACL.Arn

  # CloudWatch Alarm for Rate Limit Violations
  RateLimitAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: API-Rate-Limit-Exceeded
      AlarmDescription: Alert when rate limits are frequently exceeded
      MetricName: Count
      Namespace: AWS/ApiGateway
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 100
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: ApiName
          Value: !Ref AnsvarAPI
      AlarmActions:
        - !Ref SNSTopic

  # SNS Topic for Alerts
  SNSTopic:
    Type: AWS::SNS::Topic
    Properties:
      TopicName: API-Security-Alerts
      Subscription:
        - Endpoint: security@ansvar.com
          Protocol: email

  # Backend Lambda (example)
  BackendLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: ansvar-api-backend
      Runtime: nodejs18.x
      Handler: index.handler
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          exports.handler = async (event) => {
              return {
                  statusCode: 200,
                  body: JSON.stringify({ message: 'Success' })
              };
          };
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn

  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - 'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole'

  # Lambda Permission for API Gateway
  LambdaPermission:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref BackendLambda
      Action: 'lambda:InvokeFunction'
      Principal: apigateway.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !Sub 'arn:aws:execute-api:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:${AnsvarAPI}/*'

Outputs:
  APIEndpoint:
    Description: API Gateway endpoint URL
    Value: !Sub 'https://${AnsvarAPI}.execute-api.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com/prod'
  
  APIKey:
    Description: API Key for authentication
    Value: !Ref StandardAPIKey
  
  UsagePlanId:
    Description: Usage Plan ID
    Value: !Ref APIUsagePlan

tools.name: AWS WAF
tools.cost: $5/month per WebACL + $1/month per rule + $0.60 per million requests

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch
tools.cost: $0.30 per alarm metric + log ingestion costs

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is maintained
validation_steps: Deploy CloudFormation stack in AWS account
Verify API Gateway throttling limits are enforced (test with load testing tool)
Test API key requirement - requests without keys should be rejected
Verify usage plan quotas are enforced (exceed daily limit)
Test WAF rate-based rule by sending high-volume requests
Attempt header injection attacks (should be blocked by WAF)
Verify CloudWatch alarms trigger when thresholds are exceeded
Review CloudWatch Logs for rate limit events
Test per-method throttling on /auth endpoints

indicators: Sudden spike in HTTP 429 responses from specific endpoints
Multiple requests from same IP with varying X-Forwarded-For headers
Unusual patterns in X-Forwarded-For headers (sequential IPs, random values)
High request volume from single source despite rate limiting
Requests with multiple proxy headers (X-Forwarded-For, X-Real-IP, X-Client-IP)
Login attempts or API calls exceeding normal user behavior patterns
Requests with malformed or suspicious IP addresses in proxy headers

log_queries.query:
// Detect rate limit bypass attempts via header manipulation
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| extend XForwardedFor = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["X-Forwarded-For"])
| extend ClientIP = CIp
| where isnotempty(XForwardedFor)
| where XForwardedFor != ClientIP
| summarize 
    RequestCount = count(),
    UniqueXFFValues = dcount(XForwardedFor),
    XFFSample = take_any(XForwardedFor)
  by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where UniqueXFFValues > 10  // Same IP using multiple X-Forwarded-For values
| order by RequestCount desc

// Detect high-frequency requests with rotating X-Forwarded-For headers
event.category: "web" AND http.response.status_code: 429
| stats count by source.ip, http.request.headers.x-forwarded-for
| where count > 50

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| eval xff=mvindex(split('X-Forwarded-For',","),0)
| eval client_ip=src_ip
| where isnotnull(xff) AND xff!=client_ip
| stats count dc(xff) as unique_xff_count by client_ip, _time span=5m
| where unique_xff_count > 10
| sort - count
| table _time, client_ip, count, unique_xff_count

fields @timestamp, @message, httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.headers.x-forwarded-for
| filter @message like /429/
| stats count() as requestCount by httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.headers.x-forwarded-for
| filter requestCount > 100
| sort requestCount desc

tools.name: Fail2Ban
tools.usage: Configure filters to detect and ban IPs attempting rate limit bypass via header manipulation

tools.name: CloudFlare Rate Limiting
tools.usage: Use CloudFlare's rate limiting rules based on verified client IP (not X-Forwarded-For)

compliance: NIST SP 800-53 (SC-5 - Denial of Service Protection)
